cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out between what times Richard could have gone to the basketball court. Let me read the information again and break it down step by step. First, the problem states that today Richard went to the basketball court. The basketball court was closed after 8pm. So whatever time he went, it must be before 8pm. Now, let's look at the other information given: 1. Richard woke up at 5am. 2. Elizabeth saw Richard taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 5am to 7am. 3. Lisa saw Richard fixing their computer at the electronic store from 3pm to 7pm. 4. Leslie saw Richard reading at the library from 7pm to 8pm. So, we need to determine what times Richard was not occupied with these activities, so he could have gone to the basketball court. Let's start by mapping out Richard's schedule throughout the day. Let me try to outline the possible times. First, Richard woke up at 5am. But then, Elizabeth saw Richard taking photos from 5am to 7am. So during that time (5am to 7am), he was at the Leaning Tower of Pisa. So he couldn't have been at the basketball court during that time. So 5am to 7am is not possible. Then, from 3pm to 7pm, Lisa saw Richard fixing a computer at the electronic store. So from 3pm to 7pm, he was occupied. Therefore, he couldn't have gone to the basketball court during that time either. So that time period is also not possible. From 7pm to 8pm, Leslie saw Richard reading at the library. Since the basketball court was closed after 8pm, and he was at the library from 7pm to 8pm, he couldn't have gone to the basketball court during that time either. So 7pm to 8pm is out. Now, what about the time between when he woke up at 5am and the next activity? He woke up at 5am, but was already seen at the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 5am to 7am. So there's no time before 5am mentioned here, but he woke up at 5am, so I assume he wasn't doing anything before that. But since the basketball court's closing time is 8pm, the possible window would be from when he's free between his activities until 8pm. So let's think about the timeline: - 5am to 7am: at Pisa → not available - Then, from 7am to 3pm: What is he doing? The information doesn't say. So this is a free time window. Could he have gone to the basketball court during this time? - 3pm to 7pm: at electronic store → not available - 7pm to 8pm: at library → not available - After 8pm: court is closed anyway So the only possible time window when Richard wasn't doing anything is from 7am to 3pm. That's the only period when none of the reported activities are happening. So between 7am and 3pm, Richard could have gone to the basketball court. Wait, but he woke up at 5am, but from 5am to 7am, he was already at Pisa. So between waking up (5am) and 7am, he was not free. But from 7am until 3pm, he was free. Then from 3pm to 7pm, he was at the electronic store. Then from 7pm to 8pm, at library. After 8pm, court is closed. So the only time window when he's free is 7am to 3pm. Therefore, he could have gone to the basketball court between 7am and 3pm. Let me check the options: (A) 3pm to 7pm → No, because he was at the electronic store during that time. (B) 5am to 7am → No, because he was taking photos during that time. (C) 7pm to 8pm → No, because he was at the library during that time.
